correctedFinding: The issue is not that all field lists are missing. Strong candidate key fields are now available from Lingxing ERP API documentation, but Task 1 still lacks real same-SKU payload evidence proving which candidate key joins all required interfaces correctly.

chainLevelValidationPosition.businessQuestion: Whether ERP APIs can support SKU-level sales statistics that the existing ERP page does not provide directly.
requiredChain: inventory or warehouse quantity by same item
chainLevelValidationPosition.task1Scope: Validate the identity/join-key gate only. Full time-window sales and inventory derivation remains q_016.
chainLevelValidationPosition.failureMeaning: If a stable same-SKU join cannot be proven, the API path is not safe for v1 statistics even if individual APIs expose sales or inventory fields.

evidenceReviewed: Repository search found no checked-in real ERP payloads or endpoint field crosswalks.
User provided Lingxing ERP documentation fields for relevant API surfaces.

availableInterfaceFieldEvidence.q008Relevance: Strong bridge between local SKU, seller SKU, ASIN, and seller/account scope.
Sales windows can likely join by seller_sku and/or asin with date.
Strong candidate bridge between platform sku/msku, internal mskuId, product grouping, seller scope, and sales volume.
Inventory-side SKU evidence exists, but must be proven against product/sales SKU fields.
Multiple bridge fields exist across source/report surfaces, but payload-level alignment is still required.

candidateInterpretation.local_sku: Strong business/local SKU candidate if it maps consistently to inventory-side sku and sales-side seller_sku/msku.
candidateInterpretation.seller_sku_or_msku: Strong listing/SKU bridge across product performance, sales statistics, and platform statistics.
candidateInterpretation.mskuId: Potentially stable platform-statistics identifier if mapped to msku and then to the canonical key.
candidateInterpretation.sku: Overloaded across platform statistics and local warehouse inventory; strong only if payloads prove identical semantics.
candidateInterpretation.asin: Scoped platform/product reference; not sufficient alone as SKU-grain canonical key unless paired with seller/platform context and proven one-to-one.
candidateInterpretation.fnsku: Likely fulfillment/inventory-side reference; preserve as provenance unless mapping is proven.
candidateInterpretation.spu_or_parent_asin: Grouping reference, not SKU-grain canonical key.
candidateInterpretation.sid_seller_id_sys_wid_wid: Scope or warehouse/source identifiers, not SKU keys by themselves.

provisionalJoinModel: platform sku/local_sku <-> local warehouse sku/fnsku scoped by sys_wid or wid

recommendedCanonicalKeyPosition.selectionRule: Prefer local_sku if payload evidence shows it is stable and maps cleanly to sales seller_sku/msku and inventory sku. Prefer sku if inventory and platform APIs prove sku is the shared stable ERP key. Preserve mskuId as platform-side stable reference if required.

conflictsOrUnknowns: It is not yet proven whether local_sku, seller_sku, msku, and sku are the same value, aliases, or different scoped identifiers.
It is not yet proven whether sku in platform statistics and sku in local warehouse inventory are semantically identical.
It is not yet proven whether asin is child ASIN, parent ASIN, or insufficiently granular for SKU-level joins in all cases.
sid/seller_id may be required to disambiguate seller-scoped values.
fnsku may be FBA/inventory-specific and may not join to sales statistics directly.
spu and parent ASIN fields are grouping keys and may create false joins if used as SKU keys.

requiredInterfaceCategories: FBA/source report payload if part of the actual v1 evidence path
chainContextRequired: sales quantity candidate such as volume, order_items, or volumeTotal
inventory or warehouse quantity candidate such as day_end_count
business date/time candidate such as r_date or date_collect
scope identifiers where needed, such as sid, seller_id, sys_wid, or wid

verdict.reason: Strong candidate SKU key fields exist, but there is not yet same-SKU cross-interface payload evidence proving the canonical join rule.
verdict.nextAction: Provide a same-SKU sample payload crosswalk across the 4-5 listed interface categories.
